proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_014 (ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse outcome).
feature_006='squamous' is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
feature_001='current' (current smokers) is associated with shorter pfs_months than 'former' or 'never' smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.757 between feature_015 and pfs_months — extremely strong positive association; mean PFS rises monotonically across deciles of feature_015 from 0.74 to 6.55 months.
Mean PFS: 4.33 (stage 0), 3.19 (stage 1), 2.15 (stage 2). One-way ANOVA F=3141, p≈0. Strong monotonic decrease.
Mean PFS adeno=3.67 vs squamous=2.81 (diff=-0.86 months). F=1523, p≈0.
Mean PFS: never=3.67, former=3.61, current=2.99. Current smokers ≈0.65 mo shorter than non-current. F=433, p=4e-187.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 (binary, ~65% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_011=1 (binary, ~25% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_016=1 (binary, ~13% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months.
feature_018=1 (binary, ~35% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months.
feature_028=1 (binary, ~5% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: f023=0 → 4.36; f023=1 → 2.92 (diff -1.44). Largest single binary main effect.
Mean PFS: f016=0 → 3.33; f016=1 → 4.11 (diff +0.78). Positive main effect.
Mean PFS: f018=0 → 3.32; f018=1 → 3.64 (diff +0.32). Modest positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable OLS adjustment, feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006, feature_001, feature_016, feature_018, feature_025, feature_017 remain independent predictors of pfs_months; most other features' marginal effects are confounded.

Among the remaining binary features (feature_002, feature_005, feature_021, feature_022, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_031), only feature_031 has a meaningful adjusted main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features with smoking/histology one-hot encoded
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R²=0.894. Significant coefficients: feature_015=+0.168/unit, feature_014=-1.10, feature_023=-1.42, feature_011=-0.91, feature_016=+0.89, feature_006_squamous=-0.72, feature_001_former=+0.55, feature_001_never=+0.55 (vs current), feature_025=+0.46, feature_018=+0.32, feature_031=-0.20, feature_017=-0.073, feature_005=-0.11. Almost all continuous lab-like features (feature_003, 004, 008, 009, 010, 013, 019, 024, 026, 029, 030, 033) and binaries feature_002, 012, 020, 021, 022, 027 have null/near-null adjusted coefficients.

Adjusted main effects on PFS (months): feature_002 −0.019 (p=0.04), feature_005 −0.110 (p<.001), feature_021 −0.005 (ns), feature_022 ~0 (ns), feature_012/020/027 ~0 (ns), feature_031 −0.20 (p<.001). Hypothesis supported: only feature_031 (and small feature_005) remain meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022=1 prevalence is dramatically higher in never-smokers than in ever-smokers, suggesting feature_022 represents a smoking-associated biomarker (e.g., an EGFR-mutation-like marker).
feature_016=1 prevalence is higher in current/former smokers than in never-smokers (opposite gradient to feature_022).
feature_028=1 prevalence is higher in never-smokers than in current smokers.

analyses.result_summary: feature_022 prevalence: never=51.3%, former=8.1%, current=3.0%. Strong inverse gradient with smoking exposure — pattern matches a never-smoker-enriched somatic alteration (EGFR-like).
feature_016 prevalence: never=2.0%, former=11.9%, current=19.7%. Smoker-enriched (consistent with PD-L1-high or smoking-driven biomarker).
feature_028 prevalence: never=10.3%, former=4.8%, current=2.6%. Never-smoker-enriched pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Likely treatment-like binary variables (whose prevalence does not depend on smoking/stage) are feature_018, feature_002, feature_023, feature_011 — biomarker-like ones are feature_016, feature_022, feature_028 — and feature_021 is smoking-correlated. We will test feature_018, feature_023, feature_011 as putative treatments and feature_016, feature_022, feature_028 as candidate effect modifiers.

analyses.code: groupby smoking and stage for each binary
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 prevalence: never=35.3%, former=35.1%, current=35.0%, stage 0/1/2 ≈ 35%/35%/34% — flat. feature_023 flat at ~65% across smoking/stage. feature_011 flat at ~25%. feature_002 flat at ~15%. These are not associated with disease characteristics → plausible candidate treatments. feature_016, feature_022, feature_028 differ by smoking → biomarkers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_018=1 (treatment candidate) is concentrated in patients with feature_016=1 (biomarker), with a large positive interaction (synergy) and little or no benefit when feature_016=0.

analyses.result_summary: Two-way interaction coefficient = +2.558, p ≈ 0. Cell means: f018=0,f016=0 → 3.33 (n=28334); f018=0,f016=1 → 3.21 (n=4122); f018=1,f016=0 → 3.33 (n=15298); f018=1,f016=1 → 5.77 (n=2246). feature_018 has essentially zero effect when feature_016=0 (Δ≈0.00) but adds ~+2.56 mo when feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 × feature_016 synergistic effect is itself modified by additional covariates; in particular feature_031, feature_028, and feature_015 modulate the size of the synergy.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_018*feature_016*MOD for several MODs
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coefficients: feature_031: −4.69, p≈0 (largest, strongly negative); feature_028: −2.89, p=4e-21 (large negative); feature_015: +0.021, p=3e-8 (small positive); feature_011: −0.35, p=0.01; smoke_never: −0.80, p=0.04; feature_023, feature_014, feature_022, hist_squamous not significant. → feature_031=1 and feature_028=1 abolish the f018×f016 benefit; older age slightly amplifies it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, the PFS benefit from feature_018=1 is large in feature_031=0 patients but absent in feature_031=1 patients.

analyses.code: groupby feature_018, feature_016, feature_031 mean PFS
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1: feature_031=0 cells: f018=0 → 3.23 (n=2271); f018=1 → 7.88 (n=1237); diff +4.65. feature_031=1 cells: f018=0 → 3.19 (n=1851); f018=1 → 3.18 (n=1009); diff −0.01. The treatment effect is essentially confined to feature_031=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among feature_018=1, feature_016=1, feature_031=0 patients, the additional presence of feature_028=1 abolishes the PFS benefit (mean PFS similar to baseline ~2-3 months).

analyses.code: 4-way table feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 x feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Cell f018=1, f016=1, f031=0, f028=0: PFS=8.09 (n=1191). Cell f018=1, f016=1, f031=0, f028=1: PFS=2.35 (n=46). The f028=1 sub-cell drops back to or below baseline despite all other 'favorable' indicators present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full responder subgroup for the feature_018 treatment effect is: feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0. Within this subgroup, feature_018=1 yields ~+4.8 months PFS over feature_018=0; outside this subgroup, feature_018 has no effect on PFS.

analyses.code: subset to favorable subgroup; ttest feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup (n=3370): feature_018=0 → mean PFS 3.26 (n=2179); feature_018=1 → mean PFS 8.09 (n=1191); difference +4.83 months, t=60.95, p≈0. Outside favorable subgroup (n=46630): feature_018=0 → 3.32, feature_018=1 → 3.32, diff −0.006, t=−0.32, p=0.75. The benefit is essentially zero outside the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 treatment effect within the favorable subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0) remains ≈+4.8 months after adjustment for major prognostic factors (feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, smoking, histology, feature_025, feature_017).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_018 + adj covariates within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on feature_018 within favorable subgroup = +4.766, p≈0, n=3370. Outside subgroup adjusted coefficient = +0.001, p=0.83, n=46630. Subgroup-specific benefit is robust to confounder adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the +4.8-month feature_018 effect is consistent across all three feature_014 (stage) levels.
Within the favorable subgroup, the +4.8-month feature_018 effect is consistent across smoking categories (never/former/current).

analyses.code: stratified ttest by feature_014 within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup feature_018 effect by stage: stage 0 → +4.79 mo (n_treated=420); stage 1 → +4.93 mo (n=583); stage 2 → +4.67 mo (n=188). All highly significant; effect size essentially constant.

analyses.code: stratified ttest by feature_001 within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup feature_018 effect by smoking: never +3.99 (n_treated=23, n_untx=56); former +5.09 (n=608/1094); current +4.59 (n=560/1029). All large; relatively stable across smoking strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023=1 represents a prognostic factor (consistent shortening of PFS) rather than a treatment with response heterogeneity: the −1.4-month effect is similar across all major modifiers, with no large interactions.
feature_011=1 likewise behaves as a prognostic factor, with a stable −0.9-month main effect across modifiers.

analyses.result_summary: Only feature_023 × feature_015 (interaction coef −0.013, p=2e-25) and feature_023 × feature_014 (+0.11, p=6e-5) are significant; both are tiny per-unit modifiers. Stage-stratified effect: stage 0 −1.53, stage 1 −1.43, stage 2 −1.31 — essentially flat.

Only feature_011 × feature_015 (interaction coef −0.011, p=5e-15) is significant; small modifier. Stage-stratified effect: −1.01, −0.91, −0.84 — uniform negative effect, no responder subgroup. Behaves as a prognostic indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022=1 (the never-smoker-enriched biomarker) is associated with reduced benefit from feature_018=1; the feature_018 × feature_022 interaction is negative.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = −0.241, p=9.9e-5. Cell means: f022=0,f018=0 → 3.28 (n=28252); f022=0,f018=1 → 3.64 (n=15231); diff +0.35. f022=1,f018=0 → 3.56 (n=4204); f022=1,f018=1 → 3.67 (n=2313); diff +0.11. feature_022=1 reduces the (already modest population-average) treatment effect from +0.35 to +0.11 mo. Pattern parallels EGFR-mutant resistance to immunotherapy in real NSCLC cohorts.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028=1 has both a negative main prognostic effect and a strong negative effect modification on the feature_018+feature_016 combination — i.e., feature_028=1 patients are 'non-responders' even when other favorable conditions are met.

analyses.result_summary: feature_028 main effect = −0.81 mo. Interaction with feature_016 = −1.21 (p=4e-16); with feature_018 = −0.49 (p=4e-7); 3-way f018×f016×f028 = −2.89 (p=4e-21); with smoke_never = +0.59 (p=7e-9); with hist_squamous = −0.39 (p=4e-4). Within the otherwise favorable cell (f018=1, f016=1, f031=0), adding f028=1 drops PFS from 8.09 to 2.35 mo. Confirms feature_028 as a dual prognostic-and-resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, older feature_015 amplifies the feature_018 treatment effect (positive 3-way interaction with feature_015).

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coefficient = +0.021 per year, p=3e-8. Effect is small in magnitude (≈+0.6 mo across the 30-year age range) compared with the binary modifiers, but statistically robust. Older patients in the responder subgroup get marginally larger PFS gains from feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among other binary 'treatment-like' candidates (feature_002, feature_005, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027), there are no large treatment-by-feature interactions — these features either have no PFS effect or a small uniform main effect.

analyses.result_summary: feature_002: no interaction with any candidate modifier reaches p<0.001. feature_005: only feature_005×feature_016 is significant (coef −0.82, p=3e-6) but small absolute change because feature_005 is rare. feature_021: no interactions p<0.001 — its main effect is largely confounded by smoking status. feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 have null main and interaction effects. None defines a meaningful treatment-effect subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 has an approximately linear positive relationship with pfs_months; the relationship is large enough (≈+0.17 mo per unit, ≈+10 mo across the observed range) that feature_015 is the single strongest predictor of pfs_months and likely encodes an underlying continuous prognostic variable rather than a discrete treatment.

analyses.result_summary: Decile means rise monotonically: 0.74 → 1.55 → 2.18 → 2.66 → 3.11 → 3.63 → 4.08 → 4.63 → 5.27 → 6.55. Univariate slope +0.169 mo/unit; multivariable slope +0.168/unit (essentially unconfounded). r=0.757, p≈0. Behaves as a strong continuous prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, both former and never smoker categories carry +0.55 mo PFS advantage over current smokers (i.e., the only smoking 'penalty' is for current smokers; former and never are equivalent).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (vs current=ref): former +0.551 (p≈0), never +0.546 (p≈0). Confidence intervals overlap entirely; former and never are statistically indistinguishable, both substantially better than current smokers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond modifying the feature_018+feature_016 effect, feature_031=1 also has a small adverse main effect on pfs_months (~−0.20 mo adjusted).

analyses.result_summary: feature_031 adjusted coef = −0.204, p≈0. Independent of the responder-subgroup pathway, feature_031=1 carries a modest direct PFS penalty.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most continuous lab-like features (feature_003, 004, 008, 009, 010, 013, 019, 024, 026, 029, 030, 033) have no meaningful association with pfs_months either marginally or after adjustment — they appear to be noise / non-prognostic variables in this dataset.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate |r| < 0.01 and adjusted |coef| ~ 0 with p>0.1 for feature_003, 004, 008, 009, 010, 013, 019, 024, 026, 029, 030, 033. The only continuous features with material effects are feature_015 (+0.168), feature_017 (−0.073), feature_025 (+0.46), and feature_032 (~−0.0004 — tiny).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_025 (range 1.7-5.5) is independently associated with longer pfs_months (positive prognostic factor).
Higher feature_017 (range 0-28) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months (negative prognostic factor).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +0.463 mo per unit, p≈0. Univariate r=+0.099. Stable independent positive effect.
Adjusted coefficient = −0.073 mo per unit, p≈0 (univariate r=−0.127). Robust negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After exhaustive interaction screening for every plausible binary 'treatment' (feature_018, feature_023, feature_011, feature_028, feature_002, feature_005, feature_022, feature_021), no other treatment-effect subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>1 month) PFS benefit emerges beyond the feature_018+feature_016+feature_031=0+feature_028=0 responder subgroup already identified.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise interaction coefficients above 1.0 mo only appear for feature_018×feature_016 (+2.56), feature_016×feature_031 (−1.69), feature_028×feature_016 (−1.21). All implicate the same single treatment-effect axis. Other interactions (feature_022×feature_018, feature_005×feature_016, etc.) are statistically significant but small in absolute terms (<0.5 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious model for pfs_months is: pfs_months ≈ baseline + 0.168·feature_015 − 1.10·feature_014 − 1.42·feature_023 − 0.91·feature_011 − 0.72·(squamous) + 0.55·(former or never smoker) + 0.46·feature_025 − 0.073·feature_017 + 4.8·[feature_018=1 AND feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0] − 0.20·feature_031 (main).

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup-indicator OLS reproduces R²>0.89 with the responder-cell coefficient ≈+4.77 mo (p≈0). Other coefficients match the multivariable model. The compact model captures the vast majority of explainable variance with a single treatment-effect interaction term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for outcome pfs_months: Treatment = feature_018=1; Direction = increases pfs_months; Magnitude ≈ +4.8 months; Subgroup definition (all required) = feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0. Within this subgroup feature_018 is highly beneficial; outside it, feature_018 has no effect on PFS. The subgroup is identified by the combination of a positive predictive biomarker (feature_016), the absence of a sex-/sex-like effect modifier (feature_031=0), and the absence of a resistance/poor-prognosis factor (feature_028=0).

analyses.code: final subgroup t-test, then adjusted OLS within and outside the subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within-subgroup (n=3370): mean PFS f018=1 = 8.09 vs f018=0 = 3.26, diff +4.83 mo, t=60.95, p≈0. Adjusted coefficient on feature_018 within subgroup = +4.766 mo (p≈0). Outside subgroup (n=46630): unadjusted diff −0.006, p=0.75; adjusted diff +0.001, p=0.83. The complete responder definition is feature_018=1 ∩ feature_016=1 ∩ feature_031=0 ∩ feature_028=0; each predicate is required — removing any one collapses the effect.
